Title: Alpha-Linolenic Acid from Zanthoxylum Seed Powder Regulates Fatty Acid Metabolism and Influences Meat Quality of Pekin Duck via the ADIPOQ/AMPK/CPT‑1 Pathway
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: affects meat quality traits and energy/lipid metabolism
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes fatty acid oxidation and reduces lipid deposition/TG accumulation in duck primary myoblasts; promotes myotube differentiation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNA-seq of breast muscle from ducks fed Zanthoxylum seed powder identified ADIPOQ as a major differentially expressed and network-central gene related to fatty acid metabolism. 2. ADIPOQ overexpression promoted myotube fusion/differentiation but had little effect on myoblast proliferation. 3. ADIPOQ overexpression decreased expression of fatty acid synthesis genes (SCD1, ACC1, ACC2) and transport genes (CD36, FABP5, FATP4), while increasing fatty acid catabolism genes (CPT-1, LPL, PPARα). 4. ADIPOQ knockdown caused the opposite expression pattern and increased lipid accumulation/TG. 5. ALA increased ADIPOQ mRNA and protein and acted through the ADIPOQ/AMPK/CPT-1 pathway.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; PPI analysis; siRNA knockdown; overexpression in duck primary myoblasts; Oil Red O staining; TG assay; immunofluorescence for differentiation; western blot
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AMPK; CPT-1; PPARγ; CD36; FABP4; FASN; PPARα
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Differentially expressed in breast muscle of ducks fed different doses of Zanthoxylum seed powder; induced by ALA in primary myoblasts
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated by 60 μmol/L ALA for 48 h in duck primary myoblasts
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dietary Zanthoxylum seed powder supplementation (1.5%, 3.0%, 4.5%); ALA treatment at 60 μmol/L for 48 h
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (accession not reported); qRT-PCR validation of DEGs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High-dose Zanthoxylum seed powder/high n-3 PUFA intake may cause abnormal ADIPOQ expression and potentially adversely affect obesity risk, meat quality, and muscle yield
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for improving duck meat quality and reducing excessive intramuscular lipid deposition; dosage of feed additive should be optimized, with 1.5% Zanthoxylum seed powder suggested as preferable in this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although ADIPOQ was the main experimentally validated gene, the paper notes an inconsistency between RNA-seq trends in vivo and ALA-induced upregulation in vitro, possibly due to dose effects or mixed fatty acid composition of the feed additive.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Mitochondrial long-chain fatty acid import/oxidation module
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed entry step for mitochondrial β-oxidation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for fatty acid oxidation and thus reduces lipid deposition
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting enzyme for fatty acid β-oxidation, as stated in the discussion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: modulates lipid deposition and meat quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CPT-1 expression/protein is associated with enhanced fatty acid catabolism and reduced lipid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPT-1 was included among fatty acid catabolism markers measured after ADIPOQ manipulation. 2. ADIPOQ overexpression increased CPT-1 mRNA/protein, while ADIPOQ knockdown reduced CPT-1. 3. ALA increased ADIPOQ, AMPK/p-AMPK, and CPT-1 protein levels, supporting the ADIPOQ/AMPK/CPT-1 mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; western blot under ADIPOQ overexpression/knockdown and ALA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Downregulated by siADIPOQ; increased when ADIPOQ protein increased
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein increased after ALA treatment in duck primary myoblasts
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ALA treatment; ADIPOQ overexpression or knockdown
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway marker/target for engineering increased fatty acid oxidation and lower muscle lipid deposition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study functionally links CPT-1 mainly as a downstream pathway component of ADIPOQ/AMPK rather than as the primary manipulated gene.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AMP-activated protein kinase heterotrimer; catalytic alpha subunit detected with AMPKα-2 antibody
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: controls energy homeostasis and lipid oxidation affecting meat quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated/increased AMPK is associated with reduced lipid accumulation via enhanced fatty acid oxidation in the ADIPOQ/AMPK/CPT-1 axis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AMPK and p-AMPK protein levels increased when ADIPOQ was overexpressed and decreased when ADIPOQ was knocked down. 2. ALA treatment increased AMPK and p-AMPK together with ADIPOQ and CPT-1. 3. The paper concludes that ALA regulates lipid deposition through the ADIPOQ/AMPK/CPT-1 pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: western blot under ADIPOQ overexpression/knockdown and ALA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Protein abundance/phosphorylation positively associated with ADIPOQ abundance in duck primary myoblasts
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at protein/phosphorylation level after ALA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ALA treatment; ADIPOQ overexpression or knockdown
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: fatty acid oxidation and lipid deposition
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions as AMPK heterotrimeric kinase complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes CPT-1 pathway activity; discussion indicates AMPK activation inhibits ACC and promotes CPT-1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent central signaling node connecting ADIPOQ induction by ALA to reduced lipid deposition
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential signaling target for nutritional or molecular strategies to increase fatty acid oxidation in duck muscle
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AMPK itself was not directly genetically manipulated in this study; evidence is from protein-level pathway response downstream of ADIPOQ and ALA.
